summary: R4 fixes the repeated Chromium page-load reliability issue for the MapRenderer-owned Pixi surface. The root cause was host sizing and lifecycle timing: the empty Pixi host could have zero layout size before the canvas existed, while page-load tests could close before renderer init completed. The web shell now aborts stale mount attempts, times out pending Pixi mounts into a controlled renderer error state, keeps React-rendered error UI outside the Pixi-owned host, and clears/destroys the renderer during cleanup. The map viewport now fills the positioned map surface independent of canvas children, so Pixi receives deterministic dimensions before init. Playwright now waits for renderer-mounted state on every page load and the repeat Chromium reproduction passes.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec prettier --write apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.tsx apps/web/src/styles/shell.css packages/map-renderer/src/index.ts tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts
tests_run.result: Formatted R4 touched files; web shell/CSS/e2e were already formatted, map-renderer formatting applied.
MapRenderer typecheck passed with abortable mount options and explicit WebGL host sizing.
Web shell typecheck passed with abortable mount lifecycle and renderer status state.

Diagnostic R4 run failed 6/6 with controlled renderer errors: MapRenderer host did not receive a renderable layout size. This confirmed the blank-surface regression was host sizing before canvas append.
Repeat Chromium reproduction passed after making .map-viewport fill the positioned map surface; 6 tests passed.
Format, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.
Architecture check, sim determinism, root Vitest suite, and web Vitest suite passed; 19 root test files/89 tests and 1 web test file/3 tests passed.
Normal Chromium Playwright run passed; 2 tests passed.

decisions: Kept React as UI intent and DOM owner only; React does not create map entity DOM/SVG or manage Pixi DisplayObjects.
Kept the visible map MapRenderer/Pixi-owned; apps/web only mounts the renderer and passes read-model snapshot, viewport, selection callback, and abort signal.
Kept map presentation formulas in packages/map-renderer.
Changed Pixi init to wait for a renderable host, use explicit width/height, and prefer WebGL only instead of depending on resizeTo host sizing during initial mount.
Added mount abort/timeout handling so failures become controlled data-renderer-status/data-renderer-error state promptly rather than an empty surface.
Moved the React-rendered mount error paragraph outside the Pixi-owned host so React reconciliation cannot clear imperatively appended Pixi canvas children.
risks: The M2 map continues to use the protocol-shaped client fixture pending live worker query integration, consistent with the existing non-blocking M2 risk.
pixi.js public top-level runtime still emits broad build chunks even though init prefers WebGL only; a narrower Pixi bundle remains an architecture/dependency review topic.
requested_action: review the R4 deterministic Pixi map mount fix.
